lede: A look at May 15 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Haerin and Denis Onyango, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Prime Minister of Slovakia
events.desc: Prime Minister of Slovakia Robert Fico is shot and critically injured while meeting with supporters at an event in Handlová.

events.title: May 2013 Iraq attacks
events.desc: An upsurge in violence in Iraq leaves more than 389 people dead over three days.

events.title: Jessica Watson
events.desc: Jessica Watson becomes the youngest person to sail, non-stop and unassisted around the world solo.

events.title: Arsenal F.C.
events.desc: Arsenal F.C. go an entire league campaign unbeaten in the English Premier League, joining Preston North End F.C. with the right to claim the title "The Invincibles".

events.title: Ryukyu Islands
events.desc: The Ryukyu Islands, under U.S. military governance since their conquest in 1945, revert to Japanese control.

events.title: Pope Leo XIII
events.desc: Pope Leo XIII defends workers' rights and property rights in the encyclical Rerum novarum, the beginning of modern Catholic social teaching.

events.title: Francis Baily
events.desc: Francis Baily observes "Baily's beads" during an annular eclipse.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: Bach leads the first performance of his cantata Ich bin ein guter Hirt, BWV 85, about Jesus as the Good Shepherd.

events.title: Pope Innocent IV
events.desc: Pope Innocent IV issues the papal bull ad extirpanda, which authorizes, but also limits, the torture of heretics in the Medieval Inquisition.

events.title: Valentinian II
events.desc: Emperor Valentinian II is assassinated while advancing into Gaul against the Frankish usurper Arbogast. He is found hanging in his residence at Vienne.

births.name: Angus MacLean
births.line: Canadian farmer and politician, 25th Premier of Prince Edward Island (died 2000)

deaths.text: Robert Walls, Australian footballer, coach, and sportscaster
Harry J. Capehart, American lawyer, politician, and businessperson

observances.text: Christian feast day: Athanasius of Alexandria (Coptic Church)
Christian feast day: Hallvard Vebjørnsson (Roman Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Isidore the Laborer, celebrated with festivals in various countries, the beginning of bullfighting season in Madrid.
Christian feast day: Jean-Baptiste de La Salle (Roman Catholic Church)
